{{Infobox book
| name = The Captain
| slug = lh-01-the-captain
| precedes = [[The Engineer (novel)|The Engineer]]
| follows = [[Threshold: Stories from Cradle (novel)|Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-lh-01-the-captain.jpg|250px]]
| released = 2023
| isbn = 9781959001485
| publisher = Hidden Gnome Publishing
| pages = 418
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Varic Vallenar{{!}}Archmage Varic Vallenar undergoes a massive ritual on the planet [[Nezarin]] to channel the knowledge of his alternate selves. Instead of mere spells, he is flooded with the memories of multiple lives, most ending in death and galactic catastrophe. His father, [[Benri Vallenar]], facilitates this by granting Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic the power of six Archmages, causing his magic to evolve into an unprecedented, merging style. Following this transformation, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic begins a mission to save the galaxy, starting with an infiltration of an [[Iron Legion Hive]] where he uses '[[Absolute Burial]]' to banish a newborn [[Iron King]] into a subspace anchor, which is then launched into a sun.

While working as a professor at the [[Magic Tower]], Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic's unconventional teaching methods and massive displays of power lead to a suspension. Seeking new opportunities, he uses his ability to interpret [[Aetheric symbols]] to locate '[[The Last Horizon]],' a legendary [[Zenith]] starship. His journey leads him to discover the ship after destroying a mountain with his staff, [[Eurias]]. Upon entering, he meets the ship's [[World Spirit]], [[Horizon]], and undergoes a series of intense virtual combat simulations to prove his worth. During these trials, he encounters various potential crew members—including the [[Karoshan]] [[Queen Shyrax]], the engineer [[Mell]], and the cybernetic assassin [[Omega]]—and eventually defeats a former comrade from a past life, [[Raion Raithe]], to be officially named Captain.

As Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic begins assembling his crew, including the warrior [[Sola]] (the '[[Sword]]'), he faces escalating threats from the [[Iron Legion]] and his own father's corporate interests. [[Benri Vallenar]] is eventually captured by the [[Iron King]], who seeks to use [[Benri]]'s magic and the [[Zenith]] starship for galactic domination. Amidst internal conflicts—such as [[Omega]]'s attempts to sabotage the ship and a confrontation with the [[Galactic Union]], who attempt to commandeer the vessel—Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic manages to stabilize his crew and ship. He recruits [[Mell]] as an engineer and [[Omega]] as a pilot after a tense negotiation in the ship's high-security [[vault]].

The conflict reaches a climax when the crew launches a massive assault on the [[Grand Hive]]. Although they successfully decimate the [[Legion]]'s defenses and seemingly kill the [[Iron King]], the villain resurrects and seizes control of [[The Last Horizon]]. In a final, desperate struggle, [[Horizon]] shares her power with Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic, elevating him to a [[Sevenfold Archmage]]. Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic uses the spell '[[Terminus Mundi]]' to isolate the [[Iron King]] in a private dimension. Despite suffering brutal injuries, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic successfully uses '[[Absolute Burial]]' to force the King out of reality. The crew then engages the resurrected King in one final battle, where [[Sola]] delivers the killing blow. With the [[Iron King]] destroyed, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic prepares for future threats from [[Solstice]] and a challenge from [[Queen Shyrax]], ordering his crew to continue gathering their team.}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Varic Vallenar{{!}}Archmage Varic Vallenar conducts a massive, expensive ritual on the planet [[Nezarin]] to use a rare astronomical phenomenon to channel the magical knowledge of alternate selves. Instead of merely gaining new spells, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic is forced to experience the full memories of multiple alternate lives, most of which end in his death and galactic catastrophe. The ritual concludes abruptly when the lights on his indicator panel go out and he loses consciousness.}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Benri Vallenar]] performs a massive ritual to grant his son, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic, the magical power of six Archmages, but the spell evolves unexpectedly as the different magical styles begin to merge and learn. Upon waking, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic displays unprecedented skill by effortlessly bypassing advanced security measures and anti-magic fields. After explaining that the magic came accompanied by the memories of six different lives, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic departs for his ship, stating his intention to save the galaxy.}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|One year after his previous deaths, [[Varic Vallenar]] infiltrates an [[Iron Legion Hive]] to stop the birth of a new [[Iron King]]. Using his starship, [[Moonfall]], and powerful sealing magic, he successfully interrupts the ritual and uses an "[[Absolute Burial]]" spell to banish the newborn King into a subspace anchor. To ensure the threat is permanently destroyed, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic launches the anchor into a nearby sun, while his father, [[Benri Vallenar]], publicly claims credit for the mission to salvage the corporation's reputation.}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Varic Vallenar{{!}}Professor Varic Vallenar leads a field observation of an aurora toad hatching, using the event to attempt to teach his students how to expand and master their existing spells. After a confrontation with [[Director Porellus]] regarding his unconventional teaching methods, Vallenar performs a massive demonstration by seizing control of the weather around the [[Magic Tower]] to prove the combat utility of advanced magic. The chapter concludes with Vallenar receiving a one-month suspension for his unauthorized display of power.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After defying a magical restriction to leave the [[Magic Tower]], the protagonist reviews various high-stakes job listings via their ship's AI. While rejecting most requests as traps or trivial, they become intrigued by a search for a legendary [[Zenith Device]] known as [[The Last Horizon]]. Ultimately, the protagonist decides to use their unique ability to interpret [[Aetheric symbols]] to recreate a mysterious [[pyramid]] that may lead to the mythical starship.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After being intercepted and boarded by a [[Karoshan]] battlecruiser, the protagonist's false identity is compromised when high-grade [[Aethertech]] reveals him to be [[Varic Vallenar]]. Following a brief magical skirmish where he subdues the [[Karoshan commander]], Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic learns that [[Queen Shyrax]] has been deposed and imprisoned. As the Karoshan{{!}}Karoshans retreat, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic is immediately pursued by two hunter-ships from the [[Iron Legion]].}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|While fleeing [[Iron Legion Hunters]] using a [[Cosmic Path]] spell and a [[Subspace Drive]], the pilot of the [[Moonfall]] encounters a school of magical jelly-rays{{!}}jelly-ray creatures that damage the ship. During the chaos, a replica [[pyramid]] on board activates, signaling the proximity of the legendary ship, [[The Last Horizon]]. To survive the combined threat of the jelly-rays and the returning Hunters, the pilot uses magic to bind the creatures to the enemy ships and cast a spell to hold the crumbling hull together before attempting a desperate escape.}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After navigating a hostile, predator-filled planet, [[Varic Vallenar]] uses his powerful staff, [[Eurias]], to destroy a mountain and reveal the hidden The Zenith Starship{{!}}Zenith Starship, [[The Last Horizon]]. Upon entering, he is met by the ship's [[World Spirit]], [[Horizon]], who challenges his worthiness due to his history of failure. Despite a brief, unsettling glimpse of her manic nature, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic accepts a magical [[binding]] contract and boards the vessel to begin his trials as Captain.}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After the protagonist enters the heart of the ship and performs a ritual to become Captain, [[Horizon]] is activated but immediately forced to evade an attack by [[Iron Legion]] hunter-ships. The ship retreats into a mountain hangar for protection, where [[Horizon]] explains that she needs more crew members to unlock her full power. To find suitable candidates, the Captain agrees to enter a virtual combat simulation to vet potential recruits through a digital competition.}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Multiple characters across different settings—[[Queen Shyrax]], [[Omega]], [[Sola]], [[Mell]], and [[Raion]]—are simultaneously presented with a mysterious digital prompt offering them the chance to compete for a legendary starship called [[The Last Horizon]]. While Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax, [[Omega]], [[Mell]], and [[Raion]] all accept the invitation to enter a virtual world, [[Sola]] declines the offer to focus on her mission of killing the [[Iron Legion]].}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic undergoes a series of increasingly complex combat simulations within the [[Zenith Device]], testing his skills as a fleet commander, a starfighter pilot, and a coordinator of a massive binding spell to contain a solar dragon. After successfully navigating these scenarios, he enters a final duel against an opponent named [[Visiri]], whom he defeats using his [[Lightcaster pistol]]. The simulations conclude with the realization that the ship, [[Horizon]], is using these trials to study his capabilities and personality.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The chapter follows three different participants in a high-stakes simulation: [[Omega]], who uses his [[Aethertech body]] to playfully hunt a [[Karoshan]] warrior; [[Mell]], an engineer who unexpectedly defeats a massive mech using her [[Nova-Bots]]; and Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax, a [[Karoshan]] [[Queen]] who relentlessly proves her mastery across various disciplines, from combat to tea ceremonies. Each character navigates unique digital environments and opponents as they compete to demonstrate their worth for the The Zenith Starship{{!}}Zenith Starship.}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After enduring several exhausting rounds of combat simulations to find qualified crew members, the Captain faces a difficult battle against a highly skilled seven-person team in an opulent hall. Despite being stripped of his staff and facing intense magical and physical pressure, the Captain manages to defeat the challengers with the help of a digital assistant. The chapter concludes as the final round begins, where the Captain is unexpectedly confronted by [[Raion Raithe]], a man he recognizes from another world.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Varic Vallenar]] engages in a high-stakes combat simulation against [[Raion]], a former comrade from his past life as a [[Titan Knight]]. Despite Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic's powerful magic, the duel escalates until [[Raion]] attempts to summon his [[Divine Titan]], causing the simulation to glitch and collapse. Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic wakes on the bridge of [[The Last Horizon]] to find that he has won the fight and been named the new Captain.}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After a brutal combat trial, [[Omega]] and [[Mell]] both receive messages from the The Zenith Starship{{!}}Zenith Starship stating they have qualified for crew positions aboard [[The Last Horizon]] but have not yet been selected. [[Omega]] reacts with fury and a desire to find the Captain, while [[Mell]] receives a consolation prize in the form of rare [[Aethertech]] schematics. Meanwhile, Queen Shyrax{{!}}Shyrax learns that control of the ship is shared among the crew and decides to await her invitation while continuing her own battles.}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After [[Varic Vallenar]] is confirmed as Captain, [[Horizon]] explains the ship's specialized crew roles and establishes a pact to fulfill specific goals, leading [[Raion Raithe]] to pledge himself as the ship's Knight. Following a narrow escape from an [[Iron Legion]] battleship and a [[Subspace dive]], [[Horizon]] teleports the duo to an [[Iron Hive]] to recruit a potential '[[Sword]]' candidate known as the [[Fallen Sword]].}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Sola]] fights through a group of [[Reavers]] and [[Pawns]] to protect a small group of civilian survivors, sustaining a serious wound in the process. After falling into a chamber containing a [[Bishop]]—the [[Hive]]'s awakened consciousness—she is joined by a [[Visiri]] warrior and an [[Archmage]] who arrive to intervene. The [[Archmage]] successfully seals the [[Hive]]'s power core, and after the [[Visiri]] damages the [[Bishop]], [[Sola]] collapses from her injuries.}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After the narrator heals [[Sola]], the [[Fallen Sword]], she uses her unique resurrection magic to restore herself and her armor to perfect condition. [[Sola]], [[Raion]], and the narrator team up to fight through waves of the [[Iron Legion]] toward her docked ship while protecting a group of human survivors. Meanwhile, the [[Iron King]] regains consciousness through a specialized [[Aethertech]] device, beginning to learn about the human who previously defeated him.}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After successfully rescuing a group of civilians from an [[Iron Legion Hive]] with the help of [[Raion]] and [[Sola]], Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic uses his magic to stabilize [[Sola]]'s dilapidated war-cruiser during their escape. Once safe, [[Sola]] uses her powerful handheld cannon to destroy the remaining [[Hive]], leading to a formal introduction between the companions. Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic concludes the encounter by inviting [[Sola]] to join his crew on [[The Last Horizon]] with the ultimate goal of wiping out the [[Iron Legion]].}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|While aboard a luxury cruiser, [[Benri Vallenar]] is ambushed by the [[Iron Legion]], an army of undead and cyborg creatures. A [[Reaver]] and two Shepherds confront him with a magical contract, offering him protection under the [[Iron King]] in exchange for his cooperation. Realizing his security fleet has been defeated and he has no other choice, [[Benri]] signs the agreement and is taken away.}}
* '''Chapter 21:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After dropping off a civilian crew, the narrator successfully recruits [[Sola]] to join the crew of [[The Last Horizon]] by showing her video evidence of his past exploits and the ship's true nature. [[Sola]] agrees to join as the '[[Sword]]' of the ship under an open-ended contract, and the crew begins planning a mission to attack five [[Iron Legion]] [[Hives]] in the [[Ynthra system]]. To bolster their strength, the narrator proposes recruiting a legendary pilot named [[Tanner Hall]] before they head into combat.}}
* '''Chapter 22:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|Varic Vallenar{{!}}Professor Vallenar returns to the [[Magic Tower]] and begins a new curriculum focused on combat magic, sharing advanced techniques and personalized guidance with his students. During the lecture, he uses his experiences to teach them about the limitations of power and the importance of defining oneself through magic. The chapter concludes abruptly when Vallenar detects an internal ward breaking, signaling an imminent threat within the tower.}}
* '''Chapter 23:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After an intruder trips his personal wards, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic is confronted in his classroom by a cybernetic assassin known as [[Omega]]. Despite using powerful elemental magic and summoning war-like rabbits to combat the man's ability to slip through [[Subspace]], Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic is blasted through a window by a specialized weapon. Realizing Tower security has been compromised and his life is in immediate danger, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic calls upon [[Horizon]] for assistance.}}
* '''Chapter 24:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After the protagonist uses personal barriers to survive an assassination attempt, their starship, [[Horizon]], arrives to annihilate the attacker and capture his remains. Following the skirmish, [[Director Porellus]] attempts to blame the protagonist for the security breach and threatens detention, but he quickly backtracks when the protagonist decides to quit. The chapter concludes as [[Vallenar Corporation]] ships arrive, leading the protagonist to realize that their father is likely behind the attack.}}
* '''Chapter 25:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After being cornered by a magical barrier created by his father's starships, [[Varic Vallenar]] meets with [[Benri Vallenar]], who has arrived at the [[Magic Tower]] as a [[Galactic Union]] investigator. [[Benri]] reveals that [[Omega]]'s attack was part of a larger exposure of Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic's identity and offers him a contract for protection and corporate cooperation. Rejecting the offer to return to his father's control, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic uses magic to vanish from the ship.}}
* '''Chapter 26:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The Captain interrogates [[Omega]], a monstrous assassin known as the [[Grave Hound]], while he is held in a containment cell aboard [[The Last Horizon]]. After questioning him about his employer's intentions regarding the Captain's father, the Captain offers [[Omega]] a three-month trial period to join the crew under strict contract stipulations. The chapter concludes with the Captain deciding to use the information gathered to stage a rescue mission for his father.}}
* '''Chapter 27:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After realizing an unknown enemy is attempting to use his father as a hostage, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic uses [[The Last Horizon]] and magic to ambush a fleet of black battle cruisers attacking his father's ships. During the skirmish, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic and [[Raion]] successfully cripple the enemy flagship, leading to a boarding mission where [[Raion]] discovers a corrupted [[Iron Legion staff]]. The chapter concludes with Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic realizing the [[Iron Legion]] is behind the plot, only to find that his command to [[Horizon]] has failed.}}
* '''Chapter 28:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Sola]] and the spirit [[Horizon]] discover that the [[Iron Legion]] is strategically repositioning its [[Hives]] to launch a massive thrust into the [[Galactic Union]], led by a newly matured [[Iron King]]. Meanwhile, [[Benri Vallenar]] is captured by the [[Iron King]], who proposes a partnership to seize the [[Zenith]] starship *[[The Last Horizon]]* and use its power for galactic domination. [[Benri]] reluctantly agrees to the deal, realizing the King's ultimate goal is to utilize his magic and the [[Mirror of Silence]] to expand the [[Legion]]'s reach.}}
* '''Chapter 29:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|As the [[Iron King]]'s fleet attacks the ship, [[Omega]] escapes his prison and begins sabotaging the vessel while stealing components to build a weapon. [[Sola]] pursues him through the ship, engaging in several violent skirmishes that result in her being injured and subsequently restored by her [[Pyre]]. The conflict culminates in the drone bay, where [[Sola]] uses the [[Worldslayer]] to devastating effect, destroying [[Omega]]'s makeshift weapon and leaving him incapacitated for containment.}}
* '''Chapter 30:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The narrator uses dangerous navigational magic to accelerate the [[Vallenar Corporation]]'s ship toward [[The Last Horizon]], hoping to arrive before the [[Iron Legion]] can seize it. During the journey, [[Raion]] reports that the [[Galactic Union]] expects a massive [[Legion]] fleet at their destination, prompting the narrator to reflect on the potential loss of the ship and his own past failures. Amidst shared grief over lost comrades, the narrator resolves to reach their allies despite the looming threat of the [[Iron King]].}}
* '''Chapter 31:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After their ship's drive fails, the protagonist and [[Raion]] arrive at a colony where they discover that the [[Iron Legion]] is using the protagonist's father's magic to create indistinguishable clones for a massive invasion. The protagonist uses immense magical power to patch the colony's hull and seal out the clones, but the relief is short-lived when a massive [[Behemoth]] emerges from [[Subspace]] to attack.}}
* '''Chapter 32:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|A massive, cybernetic [[Behemoth]] made from a contrasaur attacks a colony, threatening to destroy it with its immense size and magic resistance. The protagonist uses their magic to protect the remaining colonists while [[Raion]] summons an upgraded [[Red Divine Titan]] to engage the beast. In a display of unprecedented power, [[Raion]] uses a magnified [[Combat Art]] to bore a hole through the [[Behemoth]], though the battle remains uncertain as the protagonist senses further danger.}}
* '''Chapter 33:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After Divine Titan{{!}}Raion's Divine Titan arrives at the colony and begins forcefully fusing with its pilot, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic uses his wand to cast specialized binding spell{{!}}binding spells to protect [[Raion]] from the machine's energy. Through a tense standoff and a display of magical strength, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic successfully forces the [[Titan]] to release [[Raion]] before being teleported away by [[Horizon]]. Following the battle, a recovering [[Raion]] reflects on how he survived by tapping into a profound sense of trust and friendship from Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic.}}
* '''Chapter 34:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Benri Vallenar]] reflects on the [[Iron King]]'s failed plan to capture Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic, noting that while his son showed immense magical potential, the [[Iron King]] lost a rare [[Behemoth]] to [[Raion Raithe]]'s [[Divine Titan]]. Despite the devastating destruction caused by [[The Last Horizon]] and the massive loss of [[Iron]] [[Legion ships]], the [[Iron King]] remains unfazed. He dismisses the heavy losses as insignificant, claiming the battle cost them nothing in terms of time and opportunity.}}
* '''Chapter 35:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After teleporting to the damaged ship *[[The Last Horizon]]*, the Captain discovers that the vessel sustained significant damage from an encounter with the [[Iron Legion]] and that [[Omega]] has been relocated to a secure holding facility. To repair the ship's specialized [[Aethertech]], the Captain must recruit a skilled [[Engineer]] and decides to head deeper into [[Galactic Union]] territory to find [[Mell]]. The crew plans to gather reinforcements along the way to prepare for a future confrontation with the [[Iron King]].}}
* '''Chapter 36:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After arriving near the [[Magic Tower]] to seek political support, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Professor Varic Vallenar finds the station silent and the sector under lockdown due to [[Iron Legion]] activity. A ragtag group of [[Galactic Union warships]] arrives and attempts to commandeer [[The Last Horizon]], leading Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic to confront the fleet directly. Floating in the void between ships, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic prepares to use powerful siege magic to defend his vessel against the [[Union]]'s aggressive boarding attempt.}}
* '''Chapter 37:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Commander Nelthan]] of the [[GSS Kholien]] attempts to intercept the The Zenith Starship{{!}}Zenith Starship, [[The Last Horizon]], but is confronted by Varic Vallenar{{!}}Archmage Varic Vallenar via a video call. After Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic demonstrates his immense magical power by damaging the fleet and teleporting directly onto the [[Kholien]]'s bridge to disable the crew without killing them, [[Nelthan]] realizes he cannot win. Following this display of strength, [[Nelthan]] orders his ships to release the subspace lock and allow [[The Last Horizon]] to go free.}}
* '''Chapter 38:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After escaping a [[Union]] blockade, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Captain Vallenar discovers the wreckage of the Sunsails{{!}}Sunsail and receives a cryptic message from his father's clone suggesting he contact the [[Union]]. The ship's spirit, [[Horizon]], accidentally strands the crew in [[Subspace]], leading to a violent encounter with an extra-dimensional creature. Vallenar uses magic to stabilize the [[Subspace Drive]], allowing them to return to physical space and begin planning their next move near [[Dark Space]].}}
* '''Chapter 39:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|During a tense meeting, the protagonist argues for retreating to repair their ship and recruit a crew, while [[Sola]] and [[Horizon]] advocate for an immediate fight against the [[Iron King]]. To resolve the conflict, the protagonist reveals they possess memories from five previous lives, including a failed suicide mission against the [[Grand Hive]]. This revelation convinces the crew to plan a strategic strike on the [[Hive]], utilizing a [[Pilot]] from storage to execute a high-risk [[Subspace dive]].}}
* '''Chapter 40:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The protagonist and [[Horizon]] visit the ship's secure [[vault]], a high-security prison containing dangerous, uncontainable artifacts and entities. The protagonist enters the [[vault]] to negotiate with [[Omega]], a chaotic entity held in a glass jar, attempting to recruit him for a mission against the [[Iron King]]. After a tense exchange involving failed leverage and historical storytelling, [[Omega]] considers the protagonist's ultimatum to join the crew or face destruction.}}
* '''Chapter 41:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]] signs a contract to become the new [[Pilot]] of [[The Last Horizon]], agreeing to help the crew kill their enemies in exchange for targeting [[Solstice]]. After a tense confrontation where [[Omega]]'s attempts to attack the crew are thwarted by the contract's magic, the group meets to plan a high-stakes mission to destroy the [[Grand Hive]]. Following the meeting, [[Horizon]] provides the Captain with cryptic encouragement and reveals that her magical manifestations are trophies of defeated enemies.}}
* '''Chapter 42:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Benri Vallenar]] and the [[Iron King]] observe the arrival of the ship *[[The Last Horizon]]*, which [[Benri]] suspects is a trap set by his son. The crew of the *[[Horizon]]* executes a coordinated, high-powered assault on the [[Grand Hive]] using massive elemental magic, advanced weaponry, and a specialized missile. While the attack successfully splits the planet-sized skull in half and decimates the [[Legion]]'s defenses, the narrator remains uneasy about the lack of immediate retaliation from the [[Iron King]].}}
* '''Chapter 43:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|As the [[Grand Hive]] is decimated by an assault from [[The Last Horizon]] and [[Raion Raithe]]’s [[Divine Titan]], the [[Iron King]] is sliced in half by the [[Worldslayer]]. [[Benri]] is shoved into a biological cocoon for protection, which survives a massive explosion that leaves him drifting and disoriented. After regaining consciousness briefly to realize he is alive, [[Benri]] is rescued by his son, Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic, though he reacts with resentment.}}
* '''Chapter 44:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After [[Horizon]] removes a sabotaging parasite from the protagonist's father, the group realizes that the [[Iron King]]'s death remains unconfirmed despite the destruction of the [[Grand Hive]]. A conversation regarding a prophetic flame leads [[Sola]] and the protagonist to suspect the King might return, prompting them to attempt to destroy his discarded drop-pod. The plan fails when the [[Iron King]] resurrects from the pod, overpowering [[Sola]], [[Horizon]], and the protagonist to claim control of their ship.}}
* '''Chapter 45:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|During a desperate battle, the [[Iron King]] uses his connection to the entire [[Legion]] to overwhelm Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic's magic and mental defenses. To counter this, [[Horizon]] shares her magic with Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic, revealing that his ritual has made him a [[Sevenfold Archmage]] capable of wielding her pinnacle spells. Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic uses the spell '[[Terminus Mundi]]' to pull the [[Iron King]] into a private dimension of darkness, isolating him from his [[Legion]] to engage in a final confrontation.}}
* '''Chapter 46:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|During a desperate battle in the world of [[Terminus Mundi]], the protagonist uses multiple complex spells and the entity [[Omega]] to combat the overwhelming power of the [[Iron King]]. Although the King brutally breaks both of the protagonist's arms, they successfully cast '[[Absolute Burial]],' a powerful sealing spell that forces the King out of reality. After being rescued by the ship [[Horizon]], the protagonist delivers the magical anchor of the seal to be stored in a [[vault]] to ensure the King remains imprisoned.}}
* '''Chapter 47:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After breaking free from a state of nonexistence, the [[Iron King]] is immediately confronted by Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic, [[Sola]], [[Raion Raithe]], [[Horizon]], and [[Omega]]. The group engages him in combat, culminating in Varic Vallenar{{!}}Varic using magic to create seven copies of [[Sola]] to fire the [[Worldslayer]] simultaneously. Despite a desperate attempt to disrupt the spell, the [[Iron King]] is overwhelmed and ultimately destroyed by [[Sola]]'s final strike.}}
* '''Chapter 48:''' {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|After ensuring the [[Iron King]]'s total destruction by shattering his remaining glass dome and using a matter reduction grenade, the Captain prepares for future threats from the [[Iron Legion]] and [[Solstice]]. While the crew discusses their victory, they receive a recorded challenge for leadership from [[Shyrax the Third]], [[Queen of Karosha]]. The Captain concludes the journey by ordering the crew to begin gathering the rest of their team.}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Horizon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Iron King]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Varic Vallenar]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Raion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Omega]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Sola]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Benri Vallenar]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Behemoth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Visiri]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Director Porellus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Mariala Brechess]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Raion Raithe]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Benri]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Sola Kalter]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Captain (The Last Horizon)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Queen Shyrax]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Mell]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Teranon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Bishop]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Reaver]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[King of the Iron Legion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Jak]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[King (The Last Horizon)]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Iron Legion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Galactic Union]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Subspace]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Vallenar Corporation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Subline]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Karoshan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Grand Hive]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Magic Tower]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Iron Hive]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Hive]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Pawns]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Iron Legion Hive]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Dark Space]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[D’Niss]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Titan Force]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Shepherd (The Last Horizon)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Union]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Karosha]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Dornoth IV]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Titan Knights]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Alliance (The Last Horizon)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Karoshan Alliance]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|jelly-rays}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Escalon Company]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Visiria]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Corporate Subline]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Shepherds (The Last Horizon)]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Mirror of Silence]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Absolute Burial]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aetheric symbols]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|pathfinding spell}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aetheric sigils]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Pyre]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Terminus Mundi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Cosmic Path]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Combat Artist]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Last Horizon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Eurias]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Divine Titan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Moonfall]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Subspace Drive]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Zenith Starship]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Worldslayer]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[levitation ring]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Lightcaster IV]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Zenith Device]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Zenith Devices]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Hunter-ships]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[force-blade]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|plasma pistol}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|shield amulet}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|wand}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Skimmer Drive]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Mantle (The Last Horizon)]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aether]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aethertech]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Archmage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Lichborn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Fallen Sword]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aethril]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aetheric manipulation]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Pilot]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Engineer]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Aether Technician]]}} • {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|contrasaur}}

== Sources ==
* https://openlibrary.org/works/OL33661586W

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
